channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump’s UFC event begins at the White House amid fanfare, storm threats. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The mixed martial arts fights are being held to celebrate the country’s 250th anniversary and the president’s own 80th birthday. But the forecast for the event is looking iffy.

item.title: Researchers are uncovering ADHD’s links to these other health conditions
item.description: A growing body of evidence suggests that people with ADHD may be at risk for anxiety, disordered eating, migraines, long covid and other problems.

item.title: U.S., Iran have reached a deal to end fighting, Trump and mediator Pakistan say
item.description: Trump said Sunday on Truth Social that the deal would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the U.S. naval blockade on Iranian ports. Pakistan's prime minister said an official signing ceremony will be Friday in Switzerland.

item.title: 12 presumed dead in Missouri skydiving plane crash, officials say
item.description: According to the Missouri State Highway Patrol, the plane crashed near Butler Memorial Airport, about 60 miles south of Kansas City.

item.title: I was wrong about the Knicks, and I’m giddy with joy
item.description: A coaching change was exactly what New York needed to end its 53-year NBA title drought.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Dad-in-law thinks ‘no phones’ rule at dinner shouldn’t apply to him
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Dad-in-law thinks ‘no phones’ rule at dinner shouldn’t apply to him

item.title: Here's how a Post reporter got rid of mosquitoes when nothing else worked
item.description: A D.C. neighborhood plan mimics ones used to eradicate the insects on islands. Here are strategies you can try at your home.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Play Washington Post's daily crossword puzzles online for free.

item.title: Voters are unhappy about much more than high gas prices
item.description: As the November congressional elections approach, millions of Americans — even those earning good money — are straining to make ends meet.

item.title: New York erupts in joy and disbelief as the Knicks capture their first NBA title in 53 years, beating the Spurs 94-90 in Texas
item.description: With a roster of scrappy veterans facing the young but formidable San Antonio Spurs, the Knicks shocked the world, sportsbooks and even New Yorkers, winning the series in five games. In New York, fans streamed out of bars and sidewalk watch parties to fill the streets, faces beaming with shock and ecstasy.

item.title: How this pyramid scheme scammed 10,000 victims — and what to look out for
item.description: A Texas couple was sentenced this week for their pandemic-era scam. The case illustrates how people can be more susceptible to investment scams when the economy is under strain from rising inflation.

item.title: Congress has lost its grip on funding the government
item.description: The legislature showed again this week it is really struggling to follow its own normal budget-making process, and programs Americans love could be at stake.

item.title: How President Trump's long friendship with UFC boss Dana White brought cage fighting to the White House lawn
item.description: The president and the Ultimate Fighting Championship CEO say they’ve been leaning on each other for 25 years. Now comes the fun part: a slate of MMA brawls on the South Lawn.

item.title: 1946 birthed three presidents — and remade the world
item.description: But what more can be said about them — us — now that we approach the last, inescapable hurdles?

item.title: Trump’s name is off the Kennedy Center, it confirmed in a court filing. A tarp still covers the facade.
item.description: Workers began taking letters off the performing arts center’s exterior around 3 a.m., after a judge ruled that the decision by the center’s board of trustees to rename it was illegal.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Female breadwinner tired of disrespect for her career
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Female breadwinner tired of disrespect for her career

item.title: D.C. fines Janeese Lewis George campaign, finding coordination with labor groups
item.description: The mayoral candidate's campaign vowed to overturn the agency’s order, calling it a “last-ditch effort to derail a campaign.”

item.title: The summer camp boys who reunite weekly — six decades after going home
item.description: For several years, a group of 20 Camp Saginaw alumni have been meeting up and swapping stories.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Crews begin removing Trump’s name from the Kennedy Center, reversing a dramatic change to the performing arts venue
item.description: A federal judge had ordered center officials to remove the president’s name, which was on the facade for 176 days, ruling that the decision by the center’s trustees to rename the venue was illegal. With the removal of 18 letters — “The Donald J. Trump and” — the building’s exterior will again read “The John F. Kennedy Memorial Center for the Performing Arts.”

item.title: U.S. defeats Paraguay, 4-1, in opener as World Cup returns to American soil
item.description: The U.S. men’s national team began the tournament it is co-hosting with a dominant win, overwhelming Paraguay with a first-half scoring squall that raised hopes, expectations and the noise level inside a stadium ready to believe.

item.title: U.S. forces kill leader of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua in coordinated strike, Trump says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out a strike that killed Héctor “El Niño” Guerrero, the head of the transnational Tren de Aragua gang, in an attack “coordinated closely with our friends in Venezuela,” the president said on social media, without specifying where it took place.

item.title: Parents are ‘going broke on berries’
item.description: American’s berry eating has shifted from seasonal treat to year-round habit — especially for households with young children, where a week's supply of the snack might last only until lunch.

item.title: Justice Department approves Paramount’s deal to buy Warner Bros.
item.description: Antitrust regulators at the Justice Department approved Paramount Skydance’s $110 billion deal to buy Warner Bros. Discovery, a merger that would create one of the largest companies in Hollywood and reshape the television news landscape.

item.title: One simple way to reduce back pain while sleeping
item.description: For people with chronic back pain, the discomfort doesn't end when going to sleep. Luckily for them, all that's needed is a pillow to help align their spine.

item.title: What is suffering for? After my sister died, I found the answer.
item.description: Looking on the bright side can be a mistake, Helen Andrews writes.

item.title: U.S. and Iran close to signing peace deal, officials say
item.description: After days of uncertainty and tit-for-tat military strikes, the United States and Iran have agreed to the terms of a peace deal, officials said.

item.title: Judge denies last-ditch Kennedy Center bid to delay removing Trump’s name
item.description: The denial came hours before a court-ordered deadline to remove President Donald Trump’s name from the D.C. performing arts venue.

item.title: Washington National Opera sues to force Kennedy Center to turn over $17 million in gifts
item.description: The opera filed a lawsuit Thursday alleging the Kennedy Center illegally took years' worth of donor gifts, bequests and endowment funds. The two parties cut ties in January.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Are my boyfriend’s conditions for a marriage proposal unfair?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Are my boyfriend’s conditions for a marriage proposal unfair?

item.title: SpaceX shares surge on first day of trading, securing trillionaire status for Elon Musk
item.description: Investors flocked to the company after its blockbuster market debut, lifting Musk’s net worth above $1 trillion and into the record books.

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s $1.8 billion payout fund, suggesting the government could try to resurrect it
item.description: At a hearing in Virginia federal court, U.S. District Judge Leonie M. Brinkema issued a preliminary injunction blocking the fund, which the administration sought to create for those who claim they were improperly investigated by the government.

item.title: How California became a case study in failed governance
item.description: The state’s economy is dynamic. Its government is sclerotic. And voters are starting to notice.

item.title: China and Myanmar detain U.S. citizens in separate incidents, sparking alarm
item.description: Despite no immediate evidence that they were connected, the detentions of two vocal commentators on Myanmar triggered alarm in the wider research community.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record

item.title: Qatar pursued secret talks with Iran to shield gas complex from strikes, security officials say
item.description: Qatar’s back-channel effort offers a glimpse into the hidden ways Gulf states have sought to spare themselves damage from the region’s worst war in a generation.

item.title: Iran talks. Intelligence chief. National Mall. Elon Musk. Record heat. World Cup. Berry budget. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: David Hockney, artist who made a multimillion-dollar splash, dies at 88
item.description: The painter of “A Bigger Splash” and other iconic works ranked among the most influential British artists of all time.

item.title: Kennedy Center board to fight order to remove President Donald Trump’s name as deadline looms
item.description: The Kennedy Center’s trustees voted Thursday to seek a last-minute stay of a federal judge's directive to take Trump’s name off the center’s exterior by Friday.

item.title: The midlife habits that could make or break your brain health long-term
item.description: Researchers increasingly believe the 40s, 50s and 60s represent a critical window for protecting cognitive health later in life.

item.title: Why Trump and Putin can’t escape their mistakes
item.description: Two leaders, two quagmires: How arrogance and isolation become their own trap.

item.title: Elon Musk is the world’s first trillionaire (on paper) thanks to the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Musk’s fortune depends partly on SpaceX achieving audacious goals, including putting humans on Mars. His wealth rivals that of the world’s next four richest people combined: Larry Page, Jeff Bezos, Sergey Brin and Larry Ellison.

item.title: Republican lawmakers move to officially rebrand Department of Defense as Department of War
item.description: The Trump proposal made it into early versions of the House and Senate defense policy bills, but it still faces hurdles to become law.

item.title: Park Police respond to National Mall after etchings of numbers seen as threat to Trump appear on grass
item.description: Part of the anti-Trump message “86 47″ appeared to be etched into the grass near the World War II Memorial. The numerals 8, 6 and 7 were visible, but the 4 wasn’t clear. It remains unclear how the markings were made.

item.title: Trump picks Jay Clayton, Manhattan U.S. attorney, to be director of national intelligence
item.description: President Donald Trump chose Clayton less than two weeks after he said he would appoint Bill Pulte, who has no intelligence experience, to be acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Trump says he has canceled plans to strike Iran, after vowing to hit country ‘very hard tonight’
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday withdrew threats to take Iran’s most critical oil terminal “in the not too distant future,” after fresh attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

item.title: Pentagon’s hours-long lockdown is lifted after false alarm on air-quality issue
item.description: Staff were ordered to shelter in place, but normal operations have now resumed.

item.title: Minnesota lawmaker’s killer pleads guilty, avoids potential death penalty trial
item.description: The shooting rampage last year, which left two dead and two critically injured, roiled the state and stoked concern about rising rates of politically motivated violence in the United States.

item.title: House votes against extending controversial wiretapping law that is set to lapse Friday
item.description: Lawmakers are deadlocked over Trump’s pick for the nation’s top intelligence post.

item.title: A majority of D.C. residents are unhappy about the city’s direction. We asked them why.
item.description: Some of the most common concerns are crime, the cost of living, the economy and concern about federal control, according to a Washington Post-Schar School poll.
